title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
problem_statement: Given preorder and inorder traversal arrays of a binary tree, reconstruct the original tree.

examples.explanation: The tree is reconstructed from preorder and inorder sequences.

intuition: The preorder list gives roots in sequence, and the inorder list partitions left and right subtrees around each root.

approaches.name: Recursive reconstruction with inorder index map
approaches.description: Use a hashmap to map inorder values to indices, then recursively build left and right subtrees from preorder values.
approaches.why_not: Searching for each root position in inorder would degrade to O(n^2).
interview_commentary: Preorder gives the root value for each recursive subtree build.
Inorder is used to partition left and right subtrees around the root.
A hashmap avoids repeated linear search for root positions in inorder.

interview_qa.q: Why is preorder useful for reconstruction?
interview_qa.a: The first value in preorder is the root of the whole tree or subtree.

interview_qa.q: How does inorder determine subtree size?
interview_qa.a: Elements left of the root in inorder belong to the left subtree; elements right belong to the right subtree.

interview_qa.q: Why use a hashmap for inorder indices?
interview_qa.a: It avoids repeated linear scans and keeps the algorithm linear.

interview_qa.q: What is the recursion base case?
interview_qa.a: When the left bound is greater than the right bound, there is no subtree to build.
